parentQuestion: Following a stroke, a patient ignores the left side of space — failing to dress ...

scaffolds.q: The two hemispheres of the brain have different specializations and control opposite sides of the body.
scaffolds.explain: The brain exhibits lateralization — each hemisphere controls the opposite (contralateral) side of the body. But attention and spatial awareness follow more complex rules, particularly involving the parietal lobes.

scaffolds.q: The parietal lobe is involved in spatial awareness and attention to the environment.
scaffolds.explain: The parietal lobe, particularly the posterior parietal cortex, integrates sensory information and is crucial for spatial attention, body awareness, and navigation through space. It builds your map of the world.
